  clean_names()
## Rows: 58 Columns: 25
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (2): plant_id, season
## dbl (23): N, C, cn_percent, n_molar, c_molar, cn_molar, plant_size_1, plant_...

R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com.

When you click the Knit button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

** A range of plots looking at different egg laying trends

This first plot asses larvae success as looking at the ratio of larvae compared to the number of eggs

egg_laying %>% 
  filter(season %in% c("spring", "autumn")) %>% 
  mutate(larvae_success_rate = larvae / n_eggs) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = total_leaf_area, y = larvae_success_rate, color = season)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=T)+
  labs(x = "Total leaf area",y = "Larvae success rate",title="Mapping Larvae success compared to leaf size") +
  theme_minimal() 
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 162 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 159 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

For this next graph I assesed the effects of veigtation

egg_laying %>% 
  filter(season %in% c("spring", "autumn")) %>% 
  mutate(larvae_success_rate = larvae / n_eggs) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(x=total_leaf_area, y=larvae_success_rate))+
  geom_point(mapping=aes(color=season))+
  geom_smooth(mapping=aes(color=season), method="lm", se=T)+
  labs( x = "Surrounding Vegitat",y = "Larvae success rate",
    title = "Larvae Success Rate vs. Vegitation")+
  theme_minimal()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 2 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).

egg_laying %>% 
  filter(season %in% c("spring", "autumn")) %>% 
  mutate(larvae_success_rate = larvae / n_eggs) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(x=bare_ground, y=larvae_success_rate))+
  geom_point(mapping=aes(color=season))+
  geom_smooth(mapping=aes(color=season), method="lm", se=T)+
  labs(
    x = "Bare ground (%)",
    y = "Larvae success rate",
    title = "Larvae Success Rate vs. Bare Ground"
  )+
  theme_classic()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 2 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).

egg_laying %>% 
  filter(season=="spring") %>% 
  mutate(larvae_success_rate = larvae / n_eggs) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(x=bare_ground, y=larvae_success_rate))+
  geom_point(mapping=aes(color=season))+
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=T)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).

Seporating the seasons

egg_laying %>% 
  filter(season=="spring") %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(x=bare_ground, y=predicted_n))+
  geom_point(mapping=aes(color=season))+
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=T)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Next is some of the work I did on building a correlation matrix

library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)

corr_matrix <- cor(egg_laying[, c("predicted_n", "n_se", "n_weights", "n_eggs", "larvae", "occupied", "bare_ground", "veg", "litter")], use = "complete.obs")
corr_matrix[upper.tri(corr_matrix)] <- NA
corr_long <- na.omit(as.data.frame(as.table(corr_matrix)))

ggplot(corr_long, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Freq, 2)), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "Correlation") +
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix", x = "", y = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.grid = element_blank())

title: “Butterfly breeder app” output: html_document date: “2026-03-12” —

R Markdown

plant_joined <- left_join(egg_laying, plant_chemistry %>% select(plant_id, n, c, cn_percent, n_molar, c_molar, cn_molar, quality_pc1, quality_pc2), by="plant_id")
ui <- page_fillable(
  theme = bs_theme(
    bg = "#e8f4f8",
    fg = "#2c3e50",
    primary = "#FF6B9D",
    "navbar-bg" = "#4A90E2"
  ),

  tags$head(
    tags$style(HTML("
      body {
        background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
        background-attachment: fixed;
      }
      .card {
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.95) !important;
        border-radius: 15px !important;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1) !important;
      }
      .card-header {
        background: linear-gradient(90deg, #FF6B9D 0%, #C06C84 100%) !important;
        color: white !important;
        font-weight: bold !important;
        border-radius: 15px 15px 0 0 !important;
        padding: 15px !important;
      }
      h2 {
        color: white !important;
        text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
        margin-bottom: 20px;
      }
      .butterfly-emoji {
        font-size: 3em;
        text-align: center;
        margin: 10px 0;
      }
    "))
  ),

  div(
    style = "padding: 20px;",
    h2("🦋 Butterfly Breeder App 🦋", style = "text-align: center;"),

    layout_columns(
      col_widths = c(3, 9),

      card(
        card_header("Breeding Controls"),
        div(class = "butterfly-emoji", "🦋"),

        sliderInput(
          "nitrogen_range",
          "Nitrogen Level Range:",
          min = floor(min(plant_joined$n, na.rm = TRUE)),
          max = ceiling(max(plant_joined$n, na.rm = TRUE)),
          value = c(
            floor(min(plant_joined$n, na.rm = TRUE)),
            ceiling(max(plant_joined$n, na.rm = TRUE))
          ),
          step = 0.1
        ),

        sliderInput(
          "temp_ambient_range",
          "Ambient Temperature (°C):",
          min = floor(min(plant_joined$temperature_ambient, na.rm = TRUE)),
          max = ceiling(max(plant_joined$temperature_ambient, na.rm = TRUE)),
          value = c(
            floor(min(plant_joined$temperature_ambient, na.rm = TRUE)),
            ceiling(max(plant_joined$temperature_ambient, na.rm = TRUE))
          ),
          step = 0.5
        ),

        checkboxGroupInput(
          "seasons",
          "Select Seasons:",
          choices = c("Spring" = "spring", "Autumn" = "autumn"),
          selected = c("spring", "autumn")
        )
      ),

      div(
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Egg Count vs Sward Height by Season"),
            plotOutput("main_plot", height = "450px")
          )
        ),

        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(6, 6),

          card(
            card_header("Egg Count Distribution by Season"),
            plotOutput("egg_distribution", height = "350px")
          ),

          card(
            card_header("Larvae Count Distribution by Season"),
            plotOutput("larvae_distribution", height = "350px")
          )
        ),

        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(6, 6),

          card(
            card_header("Ambient Temperature Effect on Egg Count"),
            plotOutput("temp_eggs", height = "350px")
          ),

          card(
            card_header("Leaf Size Effect on Larvae Count"),
            plotOutput("leaf_larvae", height = "350px")
          )
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {

  filtered_data <- reactive({
    plant_joined %>%
      filter(
        season %in% input$seasons,
        !is.na(n),
        !is.na(temperature_ambient),
        n >= input$nitrogen_range[1],
        n <= input$nitrogen_range[2],
        temperature_ambient >= input$temp_ambient_range[1],
        temperature_ambient <= input$temp_ambient_range[2]
      )
  })

  season_colors <- c(
    "spring" = "#90EE90",
    "autumn" = "#D2691E"
  )

  output$main_plot <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()

    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() +
        annotate(
          "text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
          label = "No data available for selected filters",
          size = 6, color = "gray50"
        ) +
        theme_void()
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = n_eggs, y = sward_height, color = season)) +
        geom_point(size = 3.5, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 1.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors) +
        labs(
          title = "Relationship Between Egg Count and Sward Height",
          x = "Egg Count",
          y = "Sward Height",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })

  output$egg_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()

    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() + theme_void()
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = n_eggs, fill = season)) +
        geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
        scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors) +
        labs(
          title = "Egg Count Frequency",
          x = "Egg Count",
          y = "Frequency",
          fill = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })

  output$larvae_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()

    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() + theme_void()
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = larvae, fill = season)) +
        geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
        scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors) +
        labs(
          title = "Larvae Count Frequency",
          x = "Larvae Count",
          y = "Frequency",
          fill = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })

  output$temp_eggs <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()

    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() + theme_void()
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = temperature_ambient, y = n_eggs, color = season)) +
        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors) +
        labs(
          title = "Ambient Temperature Impact on Eggs",
          x = "Ambient Temperature (°C)",
          y = "Egg Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })

  output$leaf_larvae <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()

    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() + theme_void()
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = average_leaf_size, y = larvae, color = season)) +
        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors) +
        labs(
          title = "Leaf Size Impact on Larvae",
          x = "Average Leaf Size",
          y = "Larvae Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
Shiny applications not supported in static R Markdown documents
